english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Hebrews

verses.latin_vulgate: Multifariam, multisque modis olim Deus loquens patribus in prophetis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πολυμερῶς καὶ πολυτρόπως πάλαι ὁ θεὸς λαλήσας τοῖς πατράσιν ἐν τοῖς προφήταις
verses.king_james: God, who at sundry times and in divers manners spake in time past unto the fathers by the prophets,

verses.latin_vulgate: novissime, diebus istis locutus est nobis in Filio, quem constituit hæredem universorum, per quem fecit et sæcula:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπ᾽ ἐσχάτων τῶν ἡμερῶν τούτων ἐλάλησεν ἡμῖν ἐν υἱῷ ὃν ἔθηκεν κληρονόμον πάντων δι᾽ οὗ καὶ τοὺς αἰῶνας ἐποίησεν
verses.king_james: Hath in these last days spoken unto us by *his* Son, whom he hath appointed heir of all things, by whom also he made the worlds;

verses.latin_vulgate: qui cum sit splendor gloriæ, et figura substantiæ ejus, portansque omnia verbo virtutis suæ, purgationem peccatorum faciens, sedet ad dexteram majestatis in excelsis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς ὢν ἀπαύγασμα τῆς δόξης καὶ χαρακτὴρ τῆς ὑποστάσεως αὐτοῦ φέρων τε τὰ πάντα τῷ ῥήματι τῆς δυνάμεως αὐτοῦ δι᾽ εαυτοῦ καθαρισμὸν ποιησάμενος τῶν ἁμαρτιῶν ημῶν, ἐκάθισεν ἐν δεξιᾷ τῆς μεγαλωσύνης ἐν ὑψηλοῖς
verses.king_james: Who being the brightness of *his* glory, and the express image of his person, and upholding all things by the word of his power, when he had by himself purged our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high;

verses.latin_vulgate: tanto melior angelis effectus, quanto differentius præ illis nomen hæreditavit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοσούτῳ κρείττων γενόμενος τῶν ἀγγέλων ὅσῳ διαφορώτερον παρ᾽ αὐτοὺς κεκληρονόμηκεν ὄνομα
verses.king_james: Being made so much better than the angels, as he hath by inheritance obtained a more excellent name than they.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui enim dixit aliquando angelorum: Filius meus es tu, ego hodie genui te? Et rursum: Ego ero illi in patrem, et ipse erit mihi in filium?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τίνι γὰρ εἶπέν ποτε τῶν ἀγγέλων υἱός μου εἶ σύ ἐγὼ σήμερον γεγέννηκά σε καὶ πάλιν ἐγὼ ἔσομαι αὐτῷ εἰς πατέρα καὶ αὐτὸς ἔσται μοι εἰς υἱόν
verses.king_james: For unto which of the angels said he at any time, Thou art my Son, this day have I begotten thee? And again, I will be to him a Father, and he shall be to me a Son?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum iterum introducit primogenitum in orbem terræ, dicit: Et adorent eum omnes angeli Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν δὲ πάλιν εἰσαγάγῃ τὸν πρωτότοκον εἰς τὴν οἰκουμένην λέγει καὶ προσκυνησάτωσαν αὐτῷ πάντες ἄγγελοι θεοῦ
verses.king_james: And again, when he bringeth in the firstbegotten into the world, he saith, And let all the angels of God worship him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ad angelos quidem dicit: Qui facit angelos suos spiritus, et ministros suos flammam ignis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πρὸς μὲν τοὺς ἀγγέλους λέγει ὁ ποιῶν τοὺς ἀγγέλους αὐτοῦ πνεύματα καὶ τοὺς λειτουργοὺς αὐτοῦ πυρὸς φλόγα
verses.king_james: And of the angels he saith, Who maketh his angels spirits, and his ministers a flame of fire.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ad Filium autem: Thronus tuus Deus in sæculum sæculi: virga æquitatis, virga regni tui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πρὸς δὲ τὸν υἱόν ὁ θρόνος σου ὁ θεός εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα τοῦ αἰῶνος ῥάβδος εὐθύτητος ἡ ῥάβδος τῆς βασιλείας σου
verses.king_james: But unto the Son *he saith*, Thy throne, O God, *is* for ever and ever: a sceptre of righteousness *is* the sceptre of thy kingdom.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dilexisti justitiam, et odisti iniquitatem: propterea unxit te Deus, Deus tuus, oleo exultationis præ participibus tuis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἠγάπησας δικαιοσύνην καὶ ἐμίσησας ἀνομίαν διὰ τοῦτο ἔχρισέν σε ὁ θεός ὁ θεός σου ἔλαιον ἀγαλλιάσεως παρὰ τοὺς μετόχους σου
verses.king_james: Thou hast loved righteousness, and hated iniquity; therefore God, *even* thy God, hath anointed thee with the oil of gladness above thy fellows.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et: Tu in principio, Domine, terram fundasti: et opera manuum tuarum sunt cæli.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καί σὺ κατ᾽ ἀρχάς κύριε τὴν γῆν ἐθεμελίωσας καὶ ἔργα τῶν χειρῶν σού εἰσιν οἱ οὐρανοί
verses.king_james: And, Thou, Lord, in the beginning hast laid the foundation of the earth; and the heavens are the works of thine hands:

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipsi peribunt, tu autem permanebis, et omnes ut vestimentum veterascent:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αὐτοὶ ἀπολοῦνται σὺ δὲ διαμένεις καὶ πάντες ὡς ἱμάτιον παλαιωθήσονται
verses.king_james: They shall perish; but thou remainest; and they all shall wax old as doth a garment;

verses.latin_vulgate: et velut amictum mutabis eos, et mutabuntur: tu autem idem ipse es, et anni tui non deficient.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὡσεὶ περιβόλαιον ἑλίξεις αὐτούς καὶ ἀλλαγήσονται σὺ δὲ ὁ αὐτὸς εἶ καὶ τὰ ἔτη σου οὐκ ἐκλείψουσιν
verses.king_james: And as a vesture shalt thou fold them up, and they shall be changed: but thou art the same, and thy years shall not fail.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ad quem autem angelorum dixit aliquando: Sede a dextris meis, quoadusque ponam inimicos tuos scabellum pedum tuorum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πρὸς τίνα δὲ τῶν ἀγγέλων εἴρηκέν ποτε κάθου ἐκ δεξιῶν μου ἕως ἂν θῶ τοὺς ἐχθρούς σου ὑποπόδιον τῶν ποδῶν σου
verses.king_james: But to which of the angels said he at any time, Sit on my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nonne omnes sunt administratorii spiritus, in ministerium missi propter eos, qui hæreditatem capient salutis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐχὶ πάντες εἰσὶν λειτουργικὰ πνεύματα εἰς διακονίαν ἀποστελλόμενα διὰ τοὺς μέλλοντας κληρονομεῖν σωτηρίαν
verses.king_james: Are they not all ministering spirits, sent forth to minister for them who shall be heirs of salvation?
